findings: No material issues identified. The illustrated adult male has one head, one long straight bill, two wings, two legs with gripping feet, and one stiff, pointed tail; its black-and-white pattern, white back patch, white wing checks, head stripes, dark eye, and rear-crown red patch agree with the field-reference photos and live sources.

The plate’s measurements, Picidae family assignment, behavior, habitat summary, palette, and field-mark text agree with the independently checked sources. Leuconotopicus villosus is an accepted scientific combination; some authorities instead use Dryobates villosus.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.

verification_sources.title: Hairy Woodpecker Identification — All About Birds
